debug_points.description: These images appear to be taken from inside a vehicle, showing extreme close-up views of the vehicle's hood/roof and windshield-mounted items. The camera appears to be mounted on the dashboard capturing the car's exterior surfaces with rental car company logos (Thrifty, Hertz, Panama tourism branding, Ricardo Perez S.A.). No road surface is visible in any of the 9 images - only vehicle surfaces, logos, and a phone/GPS mount.

These images are taken from inside a vehicle and show primarily the vehicle's windshield, dashboard mount, and promotional decals on the car's exterior. The road surface is barely visible in the background of some images, showing dark asphalt, but no road damage or potholes can be assessed from these angles.

All images show a paved road surface with concrete/asphalt pavement. The images appear to be taken from very close to the ground (possibly from a vehicle-mounted camera pointing down at an angle). The road surface shows normal concrete texture with some minor surface variations but no visible potholes or missing asphalt.

These images show a road surface captured from what appears to be a moving vehicle, resulting in significant motion blur across all frames. The visible portions show asphalt pavement with some edge deterioration and a grass verge visible in the southwest view, but no potholes or holes with missing asphalt are visible.

All 9 images show extreme motion blur from a vehicle traveling at high speed. The road surface appears to be paved asphalt with white lane markings visible in some frames, but no actual road details can be resolved due to the severe blur. No potholes or road damage can be identified.

The images show a multi-lane road surface with motion blur affecting all views. The road appears to be asphalt with some weathering and surface wear visible in the South and Southwest facing images, but no actual potholes with missing asphalt or visible depth are present. The surface shows typical aging with minor cracks and discoloration but no holes.

The images show a paved road surface with motion blur artifacts making detailed assessment difficult. The road appears to be asphalt in fair condition with some surface wear. No potholes or holes with missing asphalt are visible - the images primarily show road surface, drainage ditches with standing water, and grass shoulders.

The images show a well-maintained paved road with grass median/shoulder visible in the north-facing views. The road surface appears to be in good condition with no visible holes, missing asphalt, or significant damage. The pavement shows normal wear patterns but no actual potholes.

The images show a paved road surface with motion blur affecting most views. Images 1-3 show the road edge with a drainage ditch/shoulder area with grass and some standing water. The road surface itself appears to be intact asphalt without visible potholes or missing material.

These images appear to be taken from inside a vehicle, showing the hood/roof of a rental car with branding decals (Thrifty, Hertz, Ricardo Perez S.A., Panama tourism logo, 360 Explora Panama). The camera is positioned extremely close to the vehicle surface, making it impossible to assess the actual road surface or identify any road damage.

All 9 images show extreme motion blur making detailed assessment impossible. The road surface appears to be asphalt with painted edge lines visible in some frames, but no potholes or holes with missing asphalt can be identified due to image quality issues.

These images appear to be taken from inside a moving vehicle, resulting in severe motion blur across all frames. The road surface shows asphalt with some visible cracking and wear (images 5-7 show clearest road surface details), but no actual potholes with missing asphalt, exposed base material, or visible depth can be identified.

The images show a paved road with asphalt surface. The road appears to be in fair condition with some surface wear and minor cracking visible, particularly in images 5, 6, and 7 where the road edge meets a grass shoulder. No actual potholes with missing asphalt, exposed base material, or visible depth are present in any of the 9 images.

All images show a paved road surface with motion blur making detailed assessment difficult. The visible road sections show grey asphalt with some minor surface wear and hairline cracks visible in images 3 and 4, but no actual holes, depressions, or missing asphalt are present. The road edge/shoulder with grass is visible in images 1 and 2.
